title: QA: hook matcher regex failure silently fails open (deny hooks become no-ops)
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/hooks/mod.rs:498-535
## Violation
`matches_entry` returns `false` whenever `validate_and_match` returns
`Err`, which collapses any regex-compile failure into "this entry does
not match":
```rust
fn matches_entry(entry: &HookEntry, context: &str) -> bool {
    entry.matcher.as_ref().is_none_or(|pattern| {
        match Self::validate_and_match(pattern, context) {
            Ok(matched) => matched,
            Err(e) => {
                warn!(pattern = %pattern, error = %e, "Matcher validation failed");
                false
            }
        }
    })
}
```
For an `allow` hook this is fine. For a `PreToolUse` blocker / deny hook
this is **fail-open**: a typo in the matcher regex (e.g. `(unclosed`),
an oversized pattern, or any pattern exceeding `MAX_REGEX_SIZE` silently
becomes "no hooks ran, allow the tool call". Operators get a `warn!` log
but no enforcement.
## Mandated refactor
Distinguish hook *intent* in the type system: split `HookEntry` into
`AllowHookEntry` (fail-open on matcher error is fine) and `DenyHookEntry`
(must fail-closed). For deny hooks, validate matchers at config-load
time and refuse to start with a malformed matcher. Alternatively, surface
matcher failures via a non-default `HookResult::errors` lane that the
caller MUST inspect, and make the pre-tool-use dispatch path refuse to
run when any deny matcher failed to compile.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
